wfg_ltv_econ.workerPoolConditionDesc: Smaller colonies have mostly workers, while larger colonies house more families and independent residents, limiting the labor controlled by the government.
wfg_ltv_econ.REDISTRIBUTION_DISCLAIMER: * If the redistribute credits option is enabled, credit reserves may differ from the calculated net income due to internal reallocation.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportExportsDesc: Income from exports by this outpost or colony. Smuggling exports do not produce income. In-faction imports are %s cheaper than normal
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportWagesDesc: Monthly wages for workers at this colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc1: The ratio of monthly profits that get automatically transferred to you: %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc2: The effective value can be below the chosen value if the colony is in debt.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc3: All income values are modified by this value
wfg_ltv_econ.comCellRaidObjectiveStockShortages: Shortfall between the target and current stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp1: Ship weapons, fighter LPCs, and hullmod specs. Availability is based on the "%1$s" commodity.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp2: The colony faction's doctrine affects the number of weapons vs fighter LPCs acquired. Higher ship quality increases the probability of finding modspecs..
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp3: This colony does not have heavy industry or a military presence and has no access to high-tier ship equipment.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp4: This colony has heavy industry and high-tier equipment may be found.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp5: This colony has a military presence and high-tier equipment may be found.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipOperationsDisruptedTxt: Operations disrupted! %%1$s %2$s until return to normal function.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipQueuedSection1: Click to remove or adjust position in queue
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipQueuedSection2: Currently queued for construction. Does not have any impact on the colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipNormalSection1: Currently under construction and not producing anything or providing other benefits.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipIOValuesDisclaimer: *Shown production and demand values are already adjusted based on current market size and local conditions.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipNoAICoresInstalledTxt: No AI core currently assigned. Click to assign an AI core from your cargo.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipAlphaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. Reduces upkeep cost by %2$s. Increases production by %3$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipBetaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. Reduces upkeep cost by %2$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipGammaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipImprovementDisclaimer: Each improvement made at a colony doubles the number of %1$s required to make an additional improvement.
wfg_ltv_econ.marketPolicyExpandShipyardsDisclaimer: This policy can only be activated at the faction capital, and resources are drawn from its stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.marketPolicyCooldownTxt: Policy currently on cooldown. Available in %1$d days.
wfg_ltv_econ.intelBombardTpTxt1: %%1$s was %2$s. Destroyed %%3$s of local stockpiles and reduced population health and happiness by %%4$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.intelPolicyTpTxt1: The policy %1$s is now available on %2$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.assignWorkersTpTxt1: Assign idle workers to increase this industry's output.
wfg_ltv_econ.assignWorkersTpTxt2: The number of workers that can be assigned to an industry is determined by the colony size, and certain industries have a natural limit on how many workers they can employ. Currently, there are %1$s workers employed in %2$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.serviceSectorTpTxt1: Assign workers to public services that improve stability, growth, trade efficiency, and planetary defense.
wfg_ltv_econ.serviceSectorTpTxt2: The number of workers available for public services depends on colony size and how many are already employed in other industries. Currently, %1$s workers serve in civil departments across the colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtGrandColonies: LTV-Economy already has a scroll bar for industries. Using Grand.Colonies will break the game.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtAstralAscension: Astral Ascension is not supported by LTV-Economy. Use at your own risk.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtAOTD: Ashes of the Domain has its own economy rework. LTV-Economy is not designed to be compatible with AoTD and never will be.
wfg_ltv_econ.submarketEconCommodityOnlyTxt: Can only store economy relevant commodities
wfg_ltv_econ.submarketStockpilesTpTxt1: Commodities can be deposited into or withdrawn from the stockpiles. The displayed cargo shows up to the stockpile limit of %s or the amount currently stored, whichever is lower. Contributions update the colony's reserves immediately, and the visible cargo reflects the current stockpile limits.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiComSelectionTxt1: Ctrl + Click to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiComSelectionTxt2: Must be in range of a comm relay to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiLiftEmbargoTxt: Lift the embargo on %1$s? Colonies may resume trade.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiImposeEmbargoTxt: Impose an embargo on %1$s? Colonies cannot trade with this faction while the embargo is active. This will negatively impact relations.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiRefreshMarketsTpTxt: Synchronizes EconomyEngine markets with vanilla Economy markets. Does extra checks to make sure no data is missing.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiAllocateWorkersTpTxt: Recreates the production matrix, and recalculates worker assignments. Also does a fake advance.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiFakeAdvanceTpTxt: Advances the economy by one tick without worker assignments or updating any values.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiLogAllComCellsTpTxt: Logs all the information about the CommodityCell for each cell
wfg_ltv_econ.uiLogEconInfoTpTxt: Logs all the information about the Economy
wfg_ltv_econ.uiLogIndIOTpTxt: Logs the maps used by IndustryIOs to manage industry inputs and outputs
wfg_ltv_econ.uiAllocateShipsTpTxt: Plans and allocates ship orders for Hegemony's FactionShipInventory
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalProdTpTxt: The combined daily output of %1$s across all colonies and the informals in the Sector. Represents active industrial and informal production, excluding existing stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalDemandTpTxt: The combined daily demand of all colonies for %1$s. Demand reflects how much the sector needs to maintain standard production, growth, and stability.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalSurplusTpTxt: The amount of %1$s that the sector produced beyond what was demanded. A higher surplus means that, even after all importing markets had their needs filled, some production still remained unused.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalDeficitTpTxt: Global deficit represents the total amount of demand that remained unfulfilled on the previous day. This value does not track shortages in stockpiles and only measures demand that was not supplied on the previous day. A colony may have large reserves and still contribute to the global deficit if trade routes could not deliver enough units in time.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiSectorTradeVolumeTpTxt: Total units of %1$s traded across the sector for the last %2$s days, including both in-faction and global cargo.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiSectorTradeValueTpTxt: The total monetary value (in credits) of all %1$s trades across the entire sector for the last %2$s days. This includes both in-faction and global trade, calculated using the prices at which commodities were exchanged.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalAvgPriceTpTxt: The average price of %1$s across all markets in the sector on the previous day, weighted by the quantities traded. This provides a sector-wide benchmark price, reflecting both in-faction and out-of-faction transactions.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiSectorTradeVolatilityTpTxt: Trade Volatility for the last %1$s days. Indicates how much the daily export volume for %2$s fluctuates relative to its average.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalStockpilesTpTxt: Shows the total amount of %1$s currently stored across all markets in the sector. This value reflects available stock and does not account for daily production or consumption. High stockpiles indicate abundance, while low stockpiles signal scarcity and potential trade opportunities.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalWorkersAllocatedTpTxt: Displays the total number of workers currently assigned to producing %1$s across all markets in the sector .Workers are counted based on the output of industries producing this commodity, not the industry as a whole.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalExportsTpTxt: The total count of markets in the sector that globally exported %1$s on the previous trade cycle. Informal trade excluded.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalImportsTpTxt: The total count of markets in the sector that globally imported %1$s on the previous trade cycle. Informal trade excluded.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiInformalProdTpTxt: The amount of %1$s produced by the informal sector on the previous day.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalExportShareFactionTpTxt: Shows the percentage of total exports controlled by factions and informals. Percentages do not include in-faction trade. Values are based on the previous trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiGlobalImportShareFactionTpTxt: Shows the percentage of total imports made by factions and informals. Percentages do not include in-faction trade. Values are based on the previous trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiInFactionTradeRatioTpTxt: Shows the proportion of this commodity's total exports that are traded outside the producing faction (%1$s) versus exports consumed within the same faction (%2$s). Values are based on the previous day.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiSectorPopBreakdownTpTxt1: Shows the population of the sector with the share of each faction.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiSectorWorkforceBreakdownTpTxt: Shows the worker population of the sector with the share of each faction.
